entries.summary: Always set model parameter on Agent dispatches to avoid wasting Opus tokens on Sonnet/Haiku tasks. Route by complexity: Opus for strategy, Sonnet for implementation, Haiku for utilities.
Lessons from building v2 with parallel agents: cost waste, product improvements, future opportunities. Key finding: parallel agents reduce wall-clock time 3x but require careful cost control.
Async agent messaging (DMs, channels, inboxes) to reduce Opus invocations. Teams should resolve issues internally before escalating to Opus-tier agents.
Opus agents get Haiku assistants that filter inbox, prepare briefings, reduce Opus invocations by 80%+. Proven pattern across v3.2 and v4 sprints.
CRITICAL: All work must route through forged team agents, never bypass with generic subagents. This ensures skill templates are applied and cost tracking is accurate.
All 3 phases complete (745 tests): foundation, cost+intelligence, communication+session. REFORGE REQUESTED for v3.2 design overhaul.
v3.2 design: hard activation command, Sonnet as conduit, peer-to-peer agent comms, Opus only for strategic escalations. Reduces cost by estimated 40%.
plugin.json + package.json version must match sprint number: v4.3 → 0.4.3, v4.4 → 0.4.4. Mismatch causes plugin load failures in the harness.
v5.4 SHIPPED: WorkflowRunner, SprintLoop, BudgetEnforcer, SelfCorrection, ExecutionLog, 2914 tests. All 5 autonomy gates passed. Autonomous from v5.5+.
v5.9 SHIPPED: Resilience layer, health dashboard, 3309 tests. All backlog items done. Server startup fix documented. v6.0 gate: 7 failing test files need adapter mock fixtures.
v5.4 is last human-directed sprint (100x spend); after it ships, Claude runs autonomously to v6.0. 5 gates defined for measuring autonomous progress.
v7 north star: OpenClaw runtime, Browser Use agents, CUA agents. Zero v7 work before v6.0 stable. Hard gate enforced by BudgetEnforcer.
v6.4 autonomous loop in progress: supervised PR-based loop, $50 budget, agent-driven scoring, parallel-agent execution.
v6.4.4 shipped: autonomous loop hands-off to PR via Max/Pro plan, 247 tests. Now heading into v6.5 big UI updates.
v6.5.3 shipped: full 9-phase chain real, parallel exec+retry, SSE push, cost preview. 320 tests. Heading to v6.6.0 then v7 daemon.
v6.7.1 final state: 12 releases in one day, server boot fixes, live cycle 43a67677 running PID 53000 against real repo. Dashboard demo working. v7.0.0 spec ready, impl deferred.
Dispatching 3+ parallel subagents simultaneously cuts wall-clock time but multiplies token spend. Always estimate cost before parallelizing; prefer sequential for tasks under 5 minutes.
Test failures for missing adapter mock fixtures are the #1 blocker for new route tests. Always create a minimal in-memory SqliteAdapter in beforeEach before writing route integration tests.
